linkedin.text:
Every B2B growth system is governed by five forces. Most GTM leaders only manage two of them.

I've been building a framework called the Physics of Marketing that maps the actual forces operating on your GTM system:

• Mass — the credibility and weight of your brand/offer
• Surface Area — how much of the market you're in contact with
• Momentum — compounding pipeline and brand effects
• Friction — resistance in the buyer journey
• Escape Velocity — the momentum needed to break out

The insight isn't that these forces exist. It's that they interact as a system. You can't fix pipeline by adding surface area if your friction is too high. You can't build momentum if your mass is too low to sustain it.

Most GTM problems aren't strategy problems. They're physics problems.

I wrote the definitive guide:
linkedin.title: The Physics of Marketing: A Definitive Guide to GTM Forces

thread:
Every B2B growth system is governed by five forces.

Most GTM leaders only manage two of them.

A thread on the Physics of Marketing 🧵

The five forces:

• Mass — brand/offer credibility
• Surface Area — market contact
• Momentum — compounding effects
• Friction — buyer journey resistance
• Escape Velocity — breakout threshold

They interact as a system, not in isolation.

Most GTM problems aren't strategy problems. They're physics problems.

You can't fix pipeline by adding surface area if your friction is too high.

You can't build momentum if your mass is too low to sustain it.

I wrote the definitive guide to how these forces work — and how to diagnose which one is actually killing your growth.

https://strategnik.com/thinking/physics-of-marketing-definitive-guide/
